domain: Bedouin mizmar double-reed shawm (two coupled vibrating reeds slightly detuned by construction; one airstream drives both; beat-frequency between reeds is the characteristic 'rough' texture signal; melody = average pitch, texture = difference pitch)
specific_mechanism: Single source drives two near-but-not-equal oscillators; output contains BOTH the average frequency (melody) and the difference frequency (beat texture); too-tight tuning destroys the signal.
llm_application: MIZMAR-BEAT: pair attention heads (H_i, H_i') that share inputs and values but use slightly-offset RoPE positional bases (e.g., θ_i and θ_i × (1+ε) for small ε); output includes (a) average projection (head_i + head_i')/2 carrying the consensus signal, and (b) DIFFERENCE PROJECTION (head_i − head_i') as an explicit BEAT-FREQUENCY channel encoding fine positional ambiguity. The difference channel is consumed by a downstream gating layer as a 'texture' signal not captured by either head individually.
novelty_claim: RoPE uses geometric frequency spacing across heads (independent frequencies). Multi-head attention has independent heads. PhaseGPT (Kuramoto-coupled-oscillator attention) couples heads via Kuramoto phase coupling. R294 antikythera CRT composes K prime-period rotors. MIZMAR-BEAT differs by paired (not independent) heads + DELIBERATELY DETUNED by small ε + explicit DIFFERENCE PROJECTION as readable beat-frequency channel — the difference is information, not noise to be smoothed.
